name: list
description: List all research threads with their status (active/paused/resolved), title, and item counts. Returns a summary with id, title, status, note_count, source_count, and finding_count for each thread. Do not use this for full thread details—use show instead. Do not use this for just active threads—use digest instead.

name: show
description: Display full details of a specific research thread including all notes (with dates), sources (with URLs and descriptions), and findings (with dates). Returns the complete thread object. Do not use this for an overview of all threads—use list or digest instead.

id.description: Thread ID (slug) or prefix to match. Obtain from list output.

name: add
description: Create a new research thread with a title. The thread ID is auto-generated as a lowercase slug (spaces replaced with hyphens). Returns the generated ID and title. Use this to start tracking a new topic for ongoing research.

title.description: Research thread title. Free-form text, case preserved in display but ID is lowercased.

name: note
description: Add a dated research note to a thread. Notes are timestamped automatically with the current date. Use this to record observations, questions, or context during research. For key discoveries, use finding instead.

id.description: Thread ID (slug) or prefix to match. Obtain from list output.

name: source
description: Add a source or reference URL to a thread. Stores the URL and optional description. Use this to bookmark useful links, articles, or data sources for later review.

id.description: Thread ID (slug) or prefix to match. Obtain from list output.

url.description: Source URL to add. Must be a valid URL string.

desc.description: Optional description of the source. Defaults to empty string if not provided.

name: finding
description: Record a key finding or insight from research. Findings are timestamped automatically with the current date. Use this to capture important discoveries, conclusions, or actionable items. For general research notes, use note instead.

id.description: Thread ID (slug) or prefix to match. Obtain from list output.

name: status
description: Change the status of a research thread. Use active when continuing research, paused when temporarily shelving, or resolved when research is complete. Do not use this to create new threads—use add instead.

id.description: Thread ID (slug) or prefix to match. Obtain from list output.

status.description: New status: active (researching), paused (temporarily inactive), resolved (research complete).

name: digest
description: Get a summary of all active research threads only. Returns id, title, status, and item counts for threads with status=active. Does not return paused or resolved threads. Do not use this for full thread details—use show instead. Do not use this to see all threads—use list instead.

name: decay
description: Prune stale threads. Changes status from active to paused for threads that have no notes (never been researched). Returns a list of decayed thread IDs. Does not affect threads that already have notes or are already paused/resolved. Use list first to review before running.

name: covered
description: Show topics and URLs that have been covered in research reports. Returns all thread titles and all source URLs from the thread database. Use this before starting new research to avoid duplicating work. Default looks back 14 days (note: this returns all stored data regardless of date in current implementation).

days.description: Number of days to look back. Integer value. Defaults to 14. Note: current implementation returns all data regardless of this value.
